Build context for the LLM\n431\t        context_parts = [\n432\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n433\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n434\t        ]\n435\t\n436\t        if request.project_slug:\n437\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n438\t\n439\t        if request.topic_id:\n440\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n441\t\n442\t        # Add any additional context\n443\t        if request.context:\n444\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n445\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n446\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Build the user message\n449\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n450\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n451\t            \"\",\n452\t            *context_parts,\n453\t            \"\",\n454\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n455\t        ])\n456\t\n457\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n458\t\n459\t        # Make LLM call\n460\t        try:\n461\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n462\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n463\t                user_message=user_message,\n464\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n465\t                max_tokens=4096,\n466\t                temperature=0.7,\n467\t            )\n468\t\n469\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n470\t            return bead_body\n471\t\n472\t        except Exception as e:\n473\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n474\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n475\t\n476\t    async def create_bead(\n477\t        self,\n478\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n479\t        bead_body: str,\n480\t    ) -> str:\n481\t        \"\"\"\n482\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n483\t\n484\t        Returns the bead ID.\n485\t        \"\"\"\n486\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n487\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n488\t\n489\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n490\t        metadata = {\n491\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n492\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n493\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n494\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n495\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n496\t        }\n497\t\n498\t        if request.project_slug:\n499\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n500\t\n501\t        if request.topic_id:\n502\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n503\t\n504\t        # Build bf create command\n505\t        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n506\t        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n509\t            cmd = [\n510\t                \"bf\",\n511\t                \"create\",\n512\t                \"--title\", title,\n513\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n514\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n515\t            ]\n516\t\n517\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n518\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n519\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n520\t                if value is not None:\n521\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n522\t\n523\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n524\t                *cmd,\n525\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n526\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n527\t            )\n528\t\n529\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n530\t\n531\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n532\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n533\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n534\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n535\t\n536\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n537\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n538\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n539\t\n540\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n541\t            return bead_id\n542\t\n543\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n544\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n545\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n546\t        except Exception as e:\n547\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n548\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n549\t\n550\t    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n551\t        self,\n552\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n553\t        bead_body: str,\n554\t        bead_type: str,\n555\t    ) -> str:\n556\t        \"\"\"\n557\t        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n558\t\n559\t        Returns the bead ID.\n560\t        \"\"\"\n561\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n562\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n563\t\n564\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n565\t        metadata = {\n566\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n567\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n568\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n569\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n570\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n571\t        }\n572\t\n573\t        if request.project_slug:\n574\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n575\t\n576\t        if request.topic_id:\n577\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n578\t\n579\t        # Build bf create command\n580\t        try:\n581\t            cmd = [\n582\t                \"bf\",\n583\t                \"create\",\n584\t                \"--title\", title,\n585\t                \"--type\", bead_type,\n586\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n587\t            ]\n588\t\n589\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n590\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n591\t                if value is not None:\n592\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n593\t\n594\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n595\t                *cmd,\n596\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n597\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n598\t            )\n599\t\n600\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n601\t\n602\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n603\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n604\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n605\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n606\t\n607\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n608\t            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n609\t            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n610\t\n611\t            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n612\t            return bead_id\n613\t\n614\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n615\t            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n616\t            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n617\t        except Exception as e:\n618\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n619\t            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n620\t\n621\t    async def _create_bead_watch(\n622\t        self,\n623\t        bead_ref: str,\n624\t        project_slug: str | None,\n625\t        intent_type: str,\n626\t    ) -> None:\n627\t        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n628\t\n629\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n630\t        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_hours override.\n631\t\n632\t        Args:\n633\t            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n634\t            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n635\t            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n636\t        \"\"\"\n637\t        from ..registry import get_project\n638\t\n639\t        sla_hours = None\n640\t\n641\t        # Check for per-project SLA override\n642\t        if project_slug:\n643\t            project = get_project(project_slug)\n644\t            if project:\n645\t                sla_hours = project.get(\"sla_hours\")\n646\t                if sla_hours is not None:\n647\t                    logger.info(\n648\t                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n649\t                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n650\t                    )\n651\t\n652\t        store = await self._get_store()\n653\t        await store.create_bead_watch(\n654\t            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n655\t            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n656\t            intent_type=intent_type,\n657\t        )\n658\t        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n659\t\n660\t    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n661\t        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n662\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n663\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n664\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n665\t            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n666\t        else:\n667\t            title = utterance\n668\t\n669\t        # Add project prefix if available\n670\t        if request.project_slug:\n671\t            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n672\t\n673\t        return title\n674\t\n675\t    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n676\t        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n677\t\n678\t        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n679\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n680\t        \"\"\"\n681\t        import re\n682\t\n683\t        # Strip whitespace first\n684\t        output = output.strip()\n685\t\n686\t        if not output:\n687\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n688\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n689\t\n690\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n691\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n692\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n693\t        patterns = [\n694\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n695\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n696\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n697\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n698\t        ]\n699\t\n700\t        for pattern in patterns:\n701\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n702\t            if matches:\n703\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n704\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n705\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n706\t                return bead_id\n707\t\n708\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n709\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n710\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n711\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n712\t            return output\n713\t\n714\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n715\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n716\t        return output\n717\t\n718\t    def build_pending_card(\n719\t        self,\n720\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n721\t        bead_id: str,\n722\t        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n723\t    ) -> dict:\n724\t        \"\"\"\n725\t        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n726\t\n727\t        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n728\t        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n729\t        \"\"\"\n730\t        return {\n731\t            \"type\": \"pending\",\n732\t            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n733\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n734\t            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n735\t            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n736\t            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n737\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n738\t            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n739\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n740\t            \"metadata\": {\n741\t                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n742\t                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n743\t                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n744\t            },\n745\t        }\n746\t\n747\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n748\t        \"\"\"\n749\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n750\t\n751\t        Full flow:\n752\t        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n753\t        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n754\t        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n755\t        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n756\t\n757\t        Args:\n758\t            request: The escalate request\n759\t\n760\t        Returns:\n761\t            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n762\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n763\t        \"\"\"\n764\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n765\t\n766\t        try:\n767\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n768\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n769\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n770\t\n771\t            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n772\t            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n773\t\n774\t            if auto_approve:\n775\t                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n776\t\n777\t                # Execute directly without bead creation\n778\t                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n779\t\n780\t                # Update intent in store\n781\t                store = await self._get_store()\n782\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n783\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n784\t                    status=\"resolved\",\n785\t                )\n786\t\n787\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n788\t                return EscalateResult(\n789\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n790\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n791\t                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n792\t                    status=\"completed\",\n793\t                )\n794\t\n795\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n796\t            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n797\t            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n798\t\n799\t            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n800\t            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n801\t\n802\t            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n803\t            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n804\t\n805\t            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n806\t            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n807\t            await self._create_bead_watch(\n808\t                bead_ref=bead_id,\n809\t                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n810\t                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n811\t            )\n812\t\n813\t            # Step 6: Build pending card\n814\t            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n815\t\n816\t            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n817\t            store = await self._get_store()\n818\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n819\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n820\t                status=\"dispatched\",\n821\t            )\n822\t\n823\t            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n824\t\n825\t            return EscalateResult(\n826\t                bead_id=bead_id,\n827\t                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n828\t                pending_card=pending_card,\n829\t                status=\"created\",\n830\t            )\n831\t\n832\t        except EscalateError:\n833\t            raise\n834\t        except Exception as e:\n835\t            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n836\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n837\t\n838\t\n839\t# Global escalate handler instance\n840\t_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n841\t\n842\t\n843\tdef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n844\t    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n845\t    global _handler\n846\t    if _handler is None:\n847\t        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n848\t    return _handler\n849\t\n850\t\n851\tasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n852\t    \"\"\"\n853\t    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n854\t\n855\t    Args:\n856\t        request: The escalate request\n857\t\n858\t    Returns:\n859\t        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n860\t    \"\"\"\n861\t    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n862\t    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n863\t\n864\t\n865\tasync def handle_terminal_failure(\n866\t    intent_id: str,\n867\t    session_id: str,\n868\t    topic_id: str | None,\n869\t    failure_reason: str,\n870\t    error_type: str = \"unknown\",\n871\t    bead_ref: str | None = None,\n872\t) -> None:\n873\t    \"\"\"\n874\t    Handle a terminal failure for an intent.\n875\t\n876\t    Plan §10 The Async Path: terminal failure handling.\n877\t    - Sets intents.status = 'failed'\n878\t    - Stores failure reason in bead_watch.last_refusal_reason (if bead exists)\n879\t    - Broadcasts Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n        \"\"\"\n        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n\n        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n        context = {\n            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n        }\n\n        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n        action = context.get(\"action\")\n        if action in read_only_actions:\n            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n\n        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n        for rule in safe_mutations:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n\n        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n        for rule in manual_rules:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n            if action not in actions:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate the condition\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n\n        # Check approval workflow rules\n        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n\n        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n\n    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n\n        Supports simple conditions like:\n        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n\n        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n\n        Args:\n            condition: The condition string\n            context: Dict of available variables\n\n        Returns:\n            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n\n            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n            safe_locals = {\n                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n            }\n\n            # Evaluate condition\n            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n            return bool(result)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n        intent_type_mapping = {\n            \"action\": \"action\",\n            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n        }\n\n        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n\n        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n\n    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n\n        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n            else:\n                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n                return {\n                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n                    \"data\": {\n                        \"action\": action,\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                    },\n                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n                }\n\n        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n\n    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n\n        # Execute the delete command\n        result = await executor.execute_delete_pod(\n            pod_name=params[\"pod_name\"],\n            namespace=params[\"namespace\"],\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n        try:\n            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        try:\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", bead_type,\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_watch(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n\n        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_hours override.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..registry import get_project\n\n        sla_hours = None\n\n        # Check for per-project SLA override\n        if project_slug:\n            project = get_project(project_slug)\n            if project:\n                sla_hours = project.get(\"sla_hours\")\n                if sla_hours is not None:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n                    )\n\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        await store.create_bead_watch(\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n\n        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Strip whitespace first\n        output = output.strip()\n\n        if not output:\n            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n        patterns = [\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in patterns:\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n            if matches:\n                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n                return bead_id\n\n        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n            return output\n\n        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n        return output\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n\n            if auto_approve:\n                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n\n                # Execute directly without bead creation\n                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n\n                # Update intent in store\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    status=\"resolved\",\n                )\n\n                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n                return EscalateResult(\n                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n                    status=\"completed\",\n                )\n\n            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n\n            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n\n            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n            await self._create_bead_watch(\n                bead_ref=bead_id,\n                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            )\n\n            # Step 6: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n\n\nasync def handle_terminal_failure(\n    intent_id: str,\n    session_id: str,\n    topic_id: str | None,\n    failure_reason: str,\n    error_type: str = \"unknown\",\n    bead_ref: str | None = None,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Handle a terminal failure for an intent.\n\n    Plan §10 The Async Path: terminal failure handling.\n    - Sets intents.status = 'failed'\n    - Stores failure reason in bead_watch.last_refusal_reason (if bead exists)\n    - Broadcasts task_failed SSE event\n\n    Args:\n        intent_id: The intent that failed\n        session_id: The session ID\n        topic_id: The topic ID (optional)\n        failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n        error_type: Type of error (e.g., \"worker_crash\", \"invalid_input\")\n        bead_ref: Associated bead reference (optional)\n    \"\"\"\n    from ..session.store import get_store as get_session_store\n    from ..sse.broadcaster import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent, EventType\n\n    store = get_session_store()\n\n    logger.info(f\"Handling terminal failure for intent {intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n    # Step 1: Update intent status to 'failed'\n    await store.update_intent_status(intent_id, \"failed\")\n    logger.info(f\"Set intent {intent_id} to failed status\")\n\n    # Step 2: Store failure reason in bead_watch if bead exists\n    if bead_ref:\n        try:\n            await store.update_bead_watch_refusal(\n                bead_ref=bead_ref,\n                refusal_reason=failure_reason,\n                comment_index=-1,  # No comment index for terminal failures\n                refusal_count_add=1,\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Stored failure reason for bead {bead_ref}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to update bead_watch for {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n\n    # Step 3: Broadcast task_failed event via SSE\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    await broadcaster.broadcast(\n        SSEEvent(\n            event_type=EventType.TASK_FAILED,\n            data={\n                \"bead_ref\": bead_ref,\n                \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"failure_reason\": failure_reason,\n                \"error_type\": error_type,\n                \"message\": f\"Task failed: {failure_reason}\",\n            },\n            target_session_id=session_id,\n        )\n    )\n\n
